latest.title: New investment in ManaMind
latest.content:
1 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 6949C
Mindflair PLC
01 May 2026
1 May 2026
Mindflair plc
("Mindflair" or the "Company")
New investment in autonomous game testing company ManaMind
Mindflair plc, the AIM-quoted company focused on investing in Artificial Intelligence ("AI") related technology, is pleased to announce that Sure Valley Ventures
' ("SVV") second fund
("SVV
2
"), in which Mindflair holds an interest, has made a new investment
in ManaMind
Ltd ("ManaMind")
, a London-based autonomous game testing company.
ManaMind has raised US$1.5 million in a pre-seed funding round led by SVV, with participation from EWOR, Ascension, Syndicate Room and Heartfelt. The funding will be used to expand ManaMind's technical team, accelerate proprietary model development and sup
port its growth across key geographies.
ManaMind's platform is designed to play through games, identify bugs and generate actionable reports for development teams, helping studios reduce time spent on repetitive manual testing. The company has also secure
d design partnerships with Included Games, a mobile games studio, and Crazy Labs, a leading hypercasual developer.
Nicholas Lee, Director of Mindflair plc, commented:
"
ManaMind represents an interesting application of agentic AI in the global games industry, where quality assurance remains a significant cost and operational challenge for studios. By automating repetitive testing workflows, ManaMind has the potential to help development teams improve efficiency and game quality, and we are pleased to see SVV backing another ambitious AI business with clear commercial use cases.
"
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement. The person
who arranged for the release of this information is Nicholas Lee, a director of the Company.
Enquiries:
Mindflair plc
Nicholas Lee, Director
Tel: +44 (0) 20 3368 8961

Notes to Editors
Mindflair plc (AIM: MFAI) is an investment company providing investors with access to a portfolio of next generation technology businesses focused on AI with significant growth potential. The Company is building an investment portfolio of high-tech businesses focused on Artificial Intelligence, across such areas as Internet of Things, Cyber Security, Machine Learning, Immersive Technologies and Big Data, which the Board believes demonstrate evidence of traction and the potential for exponential growth, due to increasing global demand for development in these sectors. For further information, visit:
http://www.mindflair.tech/
.
ManaMind is a London-based company developing autonomous AI agents for game testing. Its technology is designed to help game studios automate repetitive QA workflows and improve the speed and quality of testing. For more information, visit: https://www.manamind.ai

latest.title: Disseqt AI selected for Enterprise Ireland program
latest.content:
Today 09:00
RNS Number : 6611B
Mindflair PLC
23 April 2026
23 April 2026
Mindflair plc
("Mindflair" or the "Company")
Disseqt AI selected for Enterprise Ireland
programme led by
500 Global
Mindflair plc, the AIM-quoted company focused on investing in Artificial Intelligence ("AI") related technology, is pleased to announce that Disseqt AI Limited ("Disseqt AI"), an investee company of Mindflair, has been selected by Enterprise Ireland to participate in an accelerator programme led by 500 Global, the leading US venture capital firm that has backed companies including Intercom, Canva, Reddit and Stripe.
Disseqt AI is one of eight Irish technology companies selected for the programme, alongside DevAlly, Hari, Imvizar, Jarvio, Paygentic, Talio and Veloz.
The programme is designed to connect ambitious Irish startups with world-class expertise and US market insight, with a particular focus on go-to-market strategy, investor readiness and US market validation. The selected companies are currently participating in a four-week virtual phase, after which founders will travel to Palo Alto next month for two weeks of intensive market validation, including pitch rehearsals ahead of a showcase event on 14 May 2026, investor meetings and partner events at Intercom and Carta.
According to 500 Global, the programme is intended to stress-test each company's commercial scalability against Silicon Valley expectations, challenging founders to refine their global value propositions and validate US market signals.
Mindflair has built a portfolio of 30 AI-focused companies to date and continues to support investee businesses as they scale internationally.
Nicholas Lee, Director of Mindflair plc, commented:
"
We are pleased to see Disseqt AI selected for the Enterprise Ireland
programme with
500 Global. We believe
that
this is a
powerful
endorsement of the company's potential and an important opportunity
for it
to refine its product, validate US market demand and support its international growth ambitions
."
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement. The person who arranged for the release of this information is Nicholas Lee, a director of the Company.

The Company is building an investment portfolio of high-tech businesses focused on Artificial Intelligence, across such areas as Internet of Things, Cyber Security, Machine Learning, Immersive Technologies and Big Data, which the Board believes demonstrate evidence of traction and the potential for exponential growth, due to increasing global demand for development in these sectors. For further information, visit: http://www.mindflair.tech/

Disseqt AI is an AI company focused on helping organisations unlock value from complex data and accelerate commercial decision-making. latest.title: New investment in Audrey AI
latest.content:
16 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 6606A
Mindflair PLC
16 April 2026
16 April 2026
Mindflair plc
("Mindflair" or the "Company")
New investment in AI-driven financial auditing platform
Audrey AI
Mindflair plc, the AIM-quoted company focused on investing in Artificial Intelligence ("AI") related technology, is pleased to announce that Sure Valley Ventures Fund III ("SVV3"), in which Mindflair holds an interest, has made a new investment in Audrey A
I, a Dublin-based startup
company developing
an AI platform for financial auditors.
Audrey AI has raised
US
$1.8 million (€1.5 million) in a pre-seed funding round led by Sure Valley Ventures and Delta Partners, with participation from Enterprise Ireland, Donnchadh Casey (ex-CEO Calypso), Conor Jones (ex-CBO Wayflyer), and several former auditors.
The funding w
ill be used to scale the Audrey AI platform and expand its engineering and audit specialist teams as the company grows its presence across Ireland, the UK and international markets.
Audrey AI is addressing the global financial auditing market, valued at over
US
$100 billion, which
still
remains heavily reliant on manual processes. While general
purpose AI tools often struggle with the complex tabular data and evidence workflows require
d by the
auditing
profession, Audrey AI's agentic system is designed to automate the most time
intensive aspects of an audit engagement, specifically evidence gathering and testing.
In pilot
engagement
with
T
op
10 and
T
op
20 audit firms,
reduced time spen
t
on
client data collection, validation and tests of detail
by over 85%
, while simultaneously improving audit quality. The system is designed to learn each firm's specific audit methodology
, increasing in value as it is used.
Nicholas Lee, Director of Mindflair plc, commented:
"
Audrey AI represents a compelling application of agentic AI in a high-value, highly regulated sector. By automating the manual and repetitive workflows that have historically burdened the auditing profession, Audrey AI provides a critical solution to talen
t shortages and margin pressures. We believe the company is well-positioned to become a
key
infrastructure layer for the next generation of audit firms
."
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement. The person who arranged for the release of this information is Nicholas Lee, a director of the Company.

latest.title: New investment in Agentic payments company
latest.content:
14 Apr 2026 08:44
RNS Number : 3720A
Mindflair PLC
14 April 2026
Mindflair plc
("Mindflair" or the "Company")
New
i
nvestment in Agentic
p
ayments
i
nfrastructure
c
ompany
Mindflair plc, the AIM-quoted company focused on investing in Artificial Intelligence ("AI") related technology is pleased to announce that Sure Valley Ventures Fund II ("SVV2"), in which Mindflair holds an interest, has made a new investment in Ralio Limi
ted ("Ralio"), a UK-based payments infrastructure company building technology tailored for B2B AI agents.
Ralio has raised $2.5 million in pre-seed funding in a round led by Sure Valley Ventures, with participation from Seed X, Love Ventures, Plug and Pla
y, rule30, Adeline Arts and Science, Endurance Ventures, Campus Fund, fintech business angel Alan Morgan and continued support from Antler. The funding will be used to further develop Ralio's core platform and expand its engineering team in London.
Ralio
is building a payments infrastructure
system
purpose-built for autonomous B2B AI systems. As AI agents become increasingly capable of executing financial workflows independently, existing payment systems remain designed primarily for human users, relying o
n manual authentication, approval processes and human-centric fraud controls. Ralio's platform is designed to address this gap by embedding guardrails, identity verification and audit trails directly into agentic transactions, enabling AI systems to safely
and accountably move money across payment
rails
including bank transfers, cards and stablecoins.
Nicholas Lee, Director of Mindflair plc, commented:
"
Ralio is addressing an important infrastructure challenge at the intersection of AI and financial services. As AI agents become more capable of operating autonomously, the ability for them to transact safely, compliantly and with full accountability will b
e a critical enabling layer for enterprise adoption. We believe Ralio is well positioned in
this
emerging category with significant long-term potential.
"
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement. The person who arranged for the release of this information is Nicholas Lee, a director of the Company.

latest.title: Literal Labs wins AI award
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 7049Y
Mindflair PLC
31 March 2026
31 March 2026
Mindflair plc
("Mindflair" or the "Company")
Literal Labs
w
ins
AI
a
ward
Mindflair plc, the AIM-quoted company focused on investing in Artificial Intelligence ("AI"), related technology, is pleased to
note
that Literal Labs, one of the Company's portfolio companies, has been named a winner in the AI-Research & Innovation Award
category at the Global AI Excellence Award 2025.
Literal Labs was recognised in the AI-Research & Innovation Award category as part of the Global AI Excellence Award 2025 programme.
The Global AI Excellence Award
recognises organisations advancing AI across a number of categories including integration, innovation, ethics, data, hardware and generative AI. Literal Labs is developing highly efficient logic-based AI techno
logy designed to enable faster, lower-power inference, with a particular focus on delivering AI that can run effectively on standard processors and edge devices. This recognition reflects the strength of Literal Labs' technology and its potential commercia
l relevance across a range of AI applications.
Nicholas Lee, Director of Mindflair plc, commented:
"
We are delighted to see Literal Labs recognised with the AI-Research & Innovation Award at the Global AI Excellence Award 2025. This award is a
n
endorsement of the company's technical innovation and reinforces our belief in the strength of Mindflair's AI-focused portfolio. Literal Labs is addressing important challenges around AI efficiency, speed and deployability, and we look forward to supportin
g its continued progress."
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement. The person who arranged for the release of this information is Nicholas Lee, a director of the Company.

For further information, visit: http://www.mindflair.tech/

Literal Labs
is a UK-based artificial intelligence company pioneering logic-based AI, with technology designed to deliver faster, more energy-efficient and more explainable inference than traditional neural networks, while running efficiently on standard MCUs and CPUs without the need for GPUs. company_30s.what_they_do: MFAI is a UK‑listed investment vehicle that focuses on early‑stage artificial‑intelligence companies. It raises capital from institutional investors and deploys that capital into a diversified portfolio of AI start‑ups, ranging from autonomous‑gaming to medical‑image analysis.
company_30s.why_they_matter: AI is a high‑growth sector that is reshaping many industries. By backing a basket of niche AI firms, MFAI can capture upside from multiple potential exits—IPOs, acquisitions or strategic partnerships—without having to develop technology in‑house.
company_30s.current_state: MFAI has been in a prolonged downtrend, trading near the bottom of its recent price history. The company has not yet shown revenue growth; it is primarily a vehicle for deploying capital into its portfolio. The share price sits at the lowest point of its 52‑week range, reflecting the market’s current indifference.

rns_narrative: The company has been actively investing in a handful of AI start‑ups, most notably ManaMind, Disseqt AI, Literal Labs, and Vizgard. These firms have recently earned awards, secured funding, or entered into partnership programmes.
MFAI is also engaging its investor base through webinars and regular updates, signalling a shift toward greater transparency and stakeholder involvement.
The overall picture is one of a holding company that is quietly building a pipeline of AI assets, with no single company yet delivering a major exit or revenue stream.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet anticipation – investors are waiting for the next portfolio catalyst.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A small group of traders point to the recent award wins and funding rounds as evidence that MFAI’s strategy is starting to pay off. They view the lack of public chatter as a hidden opportunity.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others warn that the company’s portfolio is still too early‑stage and that a downturn in the AI market could leave MFAI with a diluted balance sheet and no clear path to profitability.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter remains largely neutral, with occasional bullish posts but no sustained momentum. The tone is cautious, suggesting that any positive news could spark a significant shift.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat, indicating no public interest. This creates a perfect stage for a surprise catalyst to trigger a sharp move.

near_term_catalysts: A funding round or partnership announcement from one of MFAI’s portfolio companies (e.g., ManaMind or Disseqt AI) could provide the first real‑world revenue signal.
A product launch, regulatory approval, or a major client win by a portfolio firm would signal that the AI solution is market‑ready.
An earnings or capital‑raising update from MFAI itself, showing a healthy cash position or new investor commitments.
A broader AI rally or sector‑wide catalyst that lifts valuation multiples for all AI‑related listings.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden increase in trading volume, a break above the 20‑day moving average, or a positive RNS from a portfolio company. A spike in institutional buying would also be a strong confirmation.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a deep crash, with capitulation likely already complete. The high compression in the price action suggests that a mean‑reversion bounce is plausible once a catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The share sits at a deep discount and the market is largely silent, creating a perfect surprise scenario. If a portfolio company delivers a catalyst and the stock rebounds to the midpoint of its historical range, the upside could be several hundred percent, while the downside is capped because the price is already near zero.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company has no revenue and relies on external funding for its portfolio. If the AI sector stalls or if the portfolio firms fail to mature, MFAI could see a further slide, potentially to zero. The worst‑case scenario is a continued decline with no meaningful catalyst.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside far exceeds the limited downside. A move from the current low to the historical midpoint offers a multiple‑hundred‑percent return, whereas a further drop to zero represents a modest loss relative to the upside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a dip after a volume spike, allocate 10% of your discretionary portfolio to MFAI, and set a stop 5% below the recent low to protect against a brief retracement.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until you see a positive RNS from ManaMind, Disseqt AI, or a significant partnership announcement. A volume surge coupled with a bullish news item would be the trigger.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks such as delisting or bankruptcy are evident, so the setup remains bullish.

ai_insights.bottom_line: MFAI is a deeply discounted AI investment vehicle with no public buzz; if one of its portfolio companies delivers a catalyst, the stock could rebound dramatically, offering a highly asymmetric upside.
